sections.title: Platform Instructions
sections.rationale: Global and repo instructions outrank generated context and should be cacheable unless files change.

sections.title: Requirement Objective
sections.rationale: Approved mission intent is the active turn authority and must not be hidden by stale cached context.

sections.title: Authority Documents
sections.rationale: Authority docs are durable workspace context, but their ordering remains below explicit requirement intent.

sections.title: Current Working Context
sections.rationale: Selected files and recent changes are hot context for the current run and should not be reused blindly.

sections.title: Continuity And Memory
sections.rationale: Continuity can shape the run but must remain below direct requirement and hot workspace context.

sections.title: Tools, Skills, And Runtime
sections.rationale: Tool and skill availability informs execution posture but does not override human-approved scope.

sections.title: Validation And Guardrails
source_refs: Do not widen scope beyond the approved bounded contexts without explicit approval.
Keep the slice local-only and non-deployable.
sections.rationale: Validation and guardrail controls are late prompt sections because they constrain execution after scope is known.

sections.title: Expected Outputs
source_refs: Tracked latest artifacts are generated from baseline-owned APIs.
No tracked latest artifact keeps test-only identifiers.
Contract and surface-closure governance remain green after refresh.
Canonical latest artifacts are refreshed from baseline-owned APIs and no tracked latest artifact carries test-only identifiers.
sections.rationale: Expected outputs define closeout behavior, not upstream authority or context selection.

notes: HOS-03 makes prompt section precedence, volatility, and cache posture explicit instead of leaving prompt assembly as an opaque string.
The profile is advisory and descriptive; it does not grant execution authority or replace requirement approval.
HOS-03 records prompt section precedence, volatility, and cache posture for the compiled execution pack.
